title: Add native macOS config/data path resolution
action: Per D1, add a macOS-native branch to base_config_dir() and base_data_dir() (src/config/mod.rs, currently ~lines 609-635) so both resolve to $HOME/Library/Application Support (no separate roaming/local split - config_dir()/data_dir() then join PRODUCT_DIR onto that same base, exactly as the existing windows/unix branches already do). Restructure each function's #[cfg(windows)] / #[cfg(not(windows))] pair into three arms: #[cfg(windows)] (unchanged), #[cfg(target_os = "macos")] (new - $HOME/Library/Application Support, erroring the same way the windows branch does if HOME is unavailable rather than silently falling back to a relative path), #[cfg(not(any(windows, target_os = "macos")))] (the existing XDG logic, unchanged, now scoped to non-macOS Unix only). Do NOT touch home() (src/config/mod.rs ~line 689) - macOS already resolves $HOME correctly via the existing #[cfg(not(windows))] arm since macOS is unix, and D1 does not change HOME resolution, only config_dir/data_dir. Do NOT touch native_roots()/native_user_profile()/native_roaming_app_data() (Windows-only, untouched) or the token-protection cfg(unix) branch (~line 794, already correctly covers macOS unchanged per D1). Add or extend a unit test (mirroring the existing data_dir_defaults_to_home_local_share test pattern near line 963) asserting that on macOS the resolved base is exactly $HOME/Library/Application Support, guarded #[cfg(target_os = "macos")] so it only runs where meaningful.

truths: On macOS, config_dir() resolves to $HOME/Library/Application Support/herdr-go and data_dir() resolves to the same base directory joined with herdr-go - i.e. config and data are the same directory on macOS, per D1
On Windows, config_dir()/data_dir() behavior is byte-identical to before this cell (still native_roots()-derived roaming/local split)
On Linux and other non-macOS Unix, config_dir()/data_dir() behavior is byte-identical to before this cell (still XDG-derived)
home() and token-protection logic are untouched

artifacts.substantive: base_config_dir/base_data_dir have a real #[cfg(target_os = "macos")] arm resolving via $HOME/Library/Application Support, not a stub or TODO; a macOS-guarded unit test asserts it

key_links: config_dir()/data_dir() (unchanged call sites) transparently pick up the new macOS base through base_config_dir()/base_data_dir() with no other code needing to change
prohibitions: Do not change Windows or Linux/XDG path resolution behavior
Do not change home(), native_roots(), or the token-protection cfg(unix)/cfg(windows) branches
Do not add a roaming/local split for macOS - one directory for both config and data, per D1
Do not silently fall back to a relative path if HOME is unavailable on macOS - fail the same explicit way the Windows branch already does

trace.outcome: Added #[cfg(target_os = "macos")] arm to base_config_dir()/base_data_dir() resolving both to $HOME/Library/Application Support (joined with PRODUCT_DIR by config_dir()/data_dir()), matching Windows' fail-loud-on-missing-HOME behavior. Windows and non-macOS-Unix (XDG) arms are unchanged. Re-scoped the existing data_dir_defaults_to_home_local_share test off macOS (its XDG assertion is now false there) and added a macOS-gated test asserting config_dir()==data_dir()==$HOME/Library/Application Support/herdr-go.
trace.verify_output: cargo test: 97 passed, 2 passed, 3 passed, 0 failed across all suites (incl new macos_config_and_data_dir_use_application_support test, cfg-gated, not run on this Linux host). cargo clippy -D warnings: clean, no output/warnings.
